ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY REPRESENTS TO THE COURT THAT THEY ARE SUBMITTING TO THE TENTATIVE RULING WHICH THE COURT THEN ADOPTED: DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO NEGLIGENCE AND OFF CALENDAR AS TO STRICT LIABILITY AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. AS TO NEGLIGENCE, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IDENTIFIED DAVID WILSON AS A PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF FACTS SUPPORTING PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTION THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES FURTHER STATED THAT MR. WILSON WAS A BRAYTON PURCELL CLIENT, AND INCLUDED A SUMMARY OF THE KNOWLEDGE MR. WILSON POSSESSED. DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT MR. WILSON DOES NOT HAVE SUCH KNOWLEDGE. DEFENDANT IS THUS PRECLUDED FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. AS TO STRICT LIABILITY AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES, PLAINTIFF REPRESENTS THAT HE IS DISMISSING THOSE CLAIMS. MOREOVER, THE DECLARATIONS OF MR. WILSON AND MR. AY CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS DISTURBED BY DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: GISELLE S. CASEY, CSR #8098

At 2:30 p.m., counsel informs the court that Union Carbide has settled out of the Walker case. The court consolidates the Walker case with Group 1 for trial.

The plaintiff requests that the Utterback case be consolidated as well because the cancer allegation is being dropped from the complaint. After hearing argument, the court takes the matter under submission.

Pertinent pre-hardship motions in limine for the cases are discussed by court and counsel. The court will decide whether or not to consolidate Utterback on Tuesday morning (10/11/11). Jury hardshipping will be conducted that day in the afternoon.

The court takes a recess from 3:11 p.m. to 3:15 p.m.

The court will need a generic statement of the case from counsel on Tuesday morning. Counsel will not need to be present for jury hardshipping. The motion to consolidate Utterback will be served on defendants in Groups 1 and 2 as well as the Utterback defense counsel.

The cases will reconvene on Tuesday, October 11, 2011 at 10:00 a.m. for pre-hardship motions in limine, the consolidation ruling on Utterback and the generic statement of the case. Jury hardshipping will commence on that day at 1:30 p.m. Voir dire will take place on Wednesday, 10/12/11, at 9:30 a.m.

At 3:30 p.m., this matter is adjourned.

At 3:57 p.m., the court takes the bench and the proceedings commence. Counsel have elected to not hire a court reporter. Therefore, the sign-in sheets produced by the courtroom clerk will list the appearances in this matter. (The mini-minutes in IJS will reflect what's on the sign-in sheets.)

The Cronin (274286) and Gleason (275034) cases are 'stand alone' cases and will not be consolidated. There has been no motion for consolidation in the Utterback (275121) matter. Counsel dispute whether there is a pending settlement with defendant Union Carbide in the Walker case (274978). The court will not consolidate the Walker case until official confirmation is received on the settlement.

At 3:56 p.m., the court hears argument on the motion to consolidate.

At 4:03 p.m., the court makes its rulings;

Group 1: Verworn (275332), Thompson (274573), Tracy (275123), Daggs (275457) and Hoover (275131).

Group 2: Daily (275437), Harrah (275456), Sharp (275011), Sherfy (275405), Tiburcio (275415) and Veio (275443).

Group 1 will go to trial first, followed by Group 2, Gleason, Cronin and then Utterback (unless it's consolidated).

Motions in limine (in all cases) that must be ruled on before jury selection will be heard tomorrow (10/6/11) at 2:00 p.m. Jury hardshipping for Groups 1 and 2 will be conducted next Tuesday (10/11/11). Unless counsel can produce a generic juror questionnaire for hardshipping, then no questionnaire will be used at all.

Court and counsel confer on the Gleason matter. According to counsel, all motions in limine to be heard before jury selection have been ruled upon by Judge Jackson. The court acknowledges this status, but still requires counsel to appear tomorrow for the hearing.

At 4:15 p.m., this matter is adjourned.

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Timonthy Barteau, Esq., of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Scott Co. of California
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Robert Tiburcio

RULING:
DEFENDANT SCOTT CO. OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Scott Co. of California's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satsify its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing materials attributable disturbed by defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony, which was attached to defendant's reply and considered by the court without objection by plaitniff, precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant's separate statement failed to make an issue of substantial factor causation. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:

Jason C. Molinelli, Esq. of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party Albay Construction Co.

Oren Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Robert Tiburcio

RULING:
DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Defendant Albay Construction Company represents to the court that they are submitting to the tentative ruling which the Court then adopted: Defendant Albay Construction Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and off calendar as to strict liability and punitive damages. As to negligence, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories identified David Wilson as a person with knowledge of facts supporting plaintiff's contention that he was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's responses further stated that Mr. Wilson was a Brayton Purcell client, and included a summary of the knowledge Mr. Wilson possessed. Defendant's separate statement failed to demonstrate that Mr. Wilson does not have such knowledge. Defendant is thus precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to strict liability and punitive damages, plaintiff represents that he is dismissing those claims. Moreover, the declarations of Mr. Wilson and Mr. Ay create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos disturbed by defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Giselle S. Casey, CSR #8098 = (220/HEK)
